Navigation: PHP Travels is a comprehensive travel booking platform designed for testing purposes. The site features a persistent top navigation bar on every page. The navigation bar displays the PHP Travels logo on the far left, followed by horizontal menu links for Home, Hotels, Flights, Tours, Cars, Visa, Offers, and Blog. On the right side of the navigation bar, a currency selector dropdown allows switching between currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.) with country flags, a language selector dropdown offers multiple language options (English, Arabic, Spanish, French, etc.), and a user account area displays "Login" and "Signup" links for unauthenticated visitors or a user dropdown with "Dashboard" and "Logout" options for authenticated users. The home page prominently features a search widget with tabs for Hotels, Flights, Tours, and Cars, each tab revealing context-specific search fields. Below the search widget, featured listings, popular destinations, and promotional banners are displayed. After successful authentication, users can access their dashboard which displays booking history, profile settings, wallet balance, wishlist, and account management options. Sub-navigation menus appear contextually within sections such as the user dashboard sidebar (My Bookings, My Profile, Wallet, Wishlist, Reviews, Settings) and listing pages (filter sidebar on the left, sorting controls at the top). Breadcrumbs appear at the top of detail and booking pages showing the navigation path back to the listing and home pages.

Functional Overview.1. Home Page & Search: The home page displays the top navigation bar with all menu links, currency/language selectors, and login/signup options. Below the navigation, a large hero section features a background banner image with a search widget overlay. The search widget has four tabs at the top: Hotels (selected by default), Flights, Tours, and Cars, each tab highlighted when active. The Hotels tab displays fields for destination/hotel name (text input with autocomplete suggestions as the user types), Check-in date (date picker with calendar popup showing current and next month), Check-out date (date picker), number of Adults (dropdown or stepper, default 2), number of Children (dropdown or stepper, default 0), and number of Rooms (dropdown, default 1). The Flights tab displays fields for Trip Type radio buttons (One Way, Round Trip, Multi City), From city (text input with autocomplete), To city (text input with autocomplete), Departure date (date picker), Return date (date picker, disabled for one-way), Passengers count (adults, children, infants), and Class dropdown (Economy, Premium Economy, Business, First). The Tours tab displays destination (text input with autocomplete) and Travel Date (date picker) fields. The Cars tab displays Pick-up Location (text input with autocomplete), Drop-off Location (text input or "same as pick-up" checkbox), Pick-up Date and Time (date and time pickers), and Drop-off Date and Time (date and time pickers). Each tab has a prominent blue "Search" button. Required fields are validated before submission; if a required field is empty, a red validation message appears below the field. When the user fills in search criteria and clicks Search, the system queries available options and redirects to the corresponding results listing page with filters and sorting options. Below the search widget, the home page displays sections for Featured Hotels (cards with images, names, locations, star ratings, and starting prices), Popular Destinations (circular or rectangular images with destination names and counts), and promotional banners or special offers.
Functional Overview.2. User Registration: The registration page is accessible by clicking the "Signup" link in the top navigation bar or via the "Don't have an account? Signup" link on the login page. The page displays a registration form centered on the page with the heading "Sign Up" or "Create Account". The form contains the following fields: First Name (text input, required), Last Name (text input, required), Email (text input, required, validated for proper email format with "@" and domain), Password (text input, masked, required, minimum length enforced), Confirm Password (text input, masked, required, must match the Password field), and Mobile Number with a country code selector dropdown showing country flags and dial codes (e.g., "+1" for US, "+44" for UK) followed by a phone number text input field. Optional fields include Address (text input) and Country (dropdown selector). A checkbox for "I agree to the Terms and Conditions" is displayed with a clickable link to the terms page, and this checkbox must be checked to proceed. A blue "Sign Up" button appears at the bottom of the form. Upon clicking Sign Up, the system validates all inputs sequentially: checking for empty required fields (displaying red error messages like "First name is required" below each empty field), verifying the email format ("Please enter a valid email address"), checking that passwords match ("Passwords do not match"), verifying the email is not already registered ("Email already exists"), and confirming the terms checkbox is checked. If all validations pass, the system creates the account and either sends a verification email with a confirmation link or automatically logs the user in and redirects to the dashboard, depending on the site configuration. A success message "Account created successfully" appears briefly.
Functional Overview.3. User Login: The login page is accessed by clicking the "Login" link in the top navigation bar. The page displays a centered login form with the heading "Login" or "Sign In". The form contains an Email text input field (with placeholder "Email"), a Password text input field (masked, with placeholder "Password"), a "Remember Me" checkbox that keeps the session active across browser closes, and a "Forgot Password?" link positioned below the password field or near the login button. Social login options may appear as buttons or icons for "Login with Google" and "Login with Facebook" if enabled, separated from the main form by an "Or" divider line. A blue "Login" button spans the width of the form. Below the button, a link reads "Don't have an account? Signup" directing to the registration page. When the user enters credentials and clicks Login, the system authenticates against the database. On successful authentication, the user is redirected to their account dashboard or the page they were previously trying to access (stored in the session redirect URL). The navigation bar updates to show the user's name with a dropdown containing "Dashboard" and "Logout" options instead of "Login" and "Signup" links. If credentials are invalid, an error message appears below the form fields in red text, such as "Invalid email or password" or "Wrong credentials, please try again." The password field is cleared while the email field retains its value. Security features include password masking with a show/hide toggle icon, and optional CAPTCHA verification (reCAPTCHA) that activates after multiple failed login attempts to prevent brute-force attacks.
Functional Overview.4. Forgot Password: The Forgot Password page is accessed by clicking the "Forgot Password?" link on the login page. The page displays a centered form with the heading "Forgot Password" or "Reset Password" and a brief instruction text such as "Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password." The form contains a single Email text input field (with placeholder "Enter your email address") and a blue "Submit" or "Reset Password" button. A "Back to Login" link appears below the button to return to the login page. When the user enters their email and clicks Submit, the system checks if the email exists in the database. If the email is found, the system sends a password reset email containing a unique reset link with an expiration time (typically 24 hours) and displays a confirmation message on the page: "A password reset link has been sent to your email address. Please check your inbox." If the email does not exist, an error message appears: "No account found with this email address." If the email field is left empty, a validation message "Email is required" appears. When the user clicks the reset link in their email, they are taken to a Set New Password page displaying two fields: New Password (masked, with strength requirements) and Confirm New Password (masked), along with a "Reset Password" button. The system validates that both passwords match and meet minimum requirements. After successful password change, a confirmation message appears and the user is redirected to the login page with a green success banner: "Your password has been reset successfully. Please login with your new password."
Functional Overview.5. Hotels Search & Listing: The hotels listing page is displayed after submitting a hotel search from the home page or navigating via the "Hotels" link in the top navigation. The page layout consists of a left sidebar with filter controls and a main content area showing results. The top of the results area displays the search criteria summary (destination, dates, guests), the total number of results found (e.g., "25 hotels found"), and a sorting dropdown with options: "Recommended", "Price: Low to High", "Price: High to Low", "Star Rating", and "Guest Rating". The left sidebar contains collapsible filter sections: Price Range (with a dual-handle slider showing minimum and maximum values and currency amounts), Star Rating (checkboxes for 1 through 5 stars with count indicators), Facilities (checkboxes for amenities like WiFi, Pool, Parking, Restaurant, Gym, Spa, with counts), Hotel Type (checkboxes for categories like Hotel, Resort, Hostel, Apartment, Villa), and Board Basis (checkboxes for options like Room Only, Breakfast Included, Half Board, Full Board, All Inclusive). Each filter section displays the number of matching properties in parentheses. As users select or deselect filters, the results update dynamically without a full page reload, and the result count updates accordingly. An "Apply Filters" or "Clear All" button may appear at the top of the sidebar. Each hotel card in the results displays a thumbnail image on the left, the hotel name as a bold clickable heading (linking to the hotel details page), the location/address text, a star rating display (e.g., 4 filled stars out of 5), small icons representing key facilities (WiFi, parking, etc.), a guest rating score (e.g., "8.5/10 Excellent"), the starting price per night in the selected currency displayed prominently on the right side, and a "View Details" or "Book Now" button. Pagination controls appear at the bottom of the results with page numbers, Previous and Next buttons, and a display count indicator (e.g., "Showing 1-10 of 25").
Functional Overview.6. Hotel Details & Booking: The hotel details page is accessed by clicking a hotel name or "View Details" button from the listing page. Breadcrumbs at the top show the navigation path (Home > Hotels > [Destination] > [Hotel Name]). The page displays a photo gallery section at the top with a large main image and thumbnail strip below for browsing multiple photos, with left/right navigation arrows and a "View All Photos" option that opens a fullscreen lightbox slideshow. Below the gallery, the hotel name appears as a large heading with the star rating, location address, and a "View on Map" link that shows a Google Maps embed with the hotel pinned. A detailed description section provides the full hotel description text. The Facilities & Amenities section displays icons with labels grouped into categories (General, Room Features, Services, Recreation). The Room Types & Availability section shows a table or card layout of available rooms, each displaying the room type name (Standard, Deluxe, Suite, etc.), a small room image, maximum occupancy icons (adults and children), room size in square meters/feet, a list of room amenities (AC, TV, minibar, safe, etc.), the price per night in the selected currency, and a "Select" or "Book Now" button. Non-available rooms show "Sold Out" in red text. The Guest Reviews section shows an aggregate rating (overall score and breakdown by categories like Cleanliness, Service, Location, Comfort, Value), the total number of reviews, and individual review cards displaying the reviewer's name, country flag, review date, stay date, rating scores, and comment text. A "Show More Reviews" button loads additional reviews. The Hotel Policies section lists check-in and check-out times, cancellation policy details, pet policy, and additional terms. When the user clicks "Select" or "Book Now" on a room, they proceed to the booking form page which displays the selected dates, room type, number of nights, and a guest information form with fields for First Name, Last Name, Email, Phone, and Special Requests (textarea). A price breakdown section shows the nightly rate, number of nights, taxes and fees, and total amount. A "Confirm Booking" or "Book Now" button at the bottom proceeds to the payment page. Login may be required before booking can be completed.
Functional Overview.7. Flights Search & Listing: The flights listing page is displayed after submitting a flight search. The page shows the search summary at the top (departure city, arrival city, dates, passengers, class) with an "Edit Search" option. The main content area lists available flights, each displayed as a horizontal card showing the airline logo and name on the left, departure time and airport code, a flight duration bar with the total time (e.g., "2h 30m") and number of stops ("Non-stop", "1 Stop", "2+ Stops") in the center, arrival time and airport code on the right, and the price per passenger prominently displayed with a "Select" or "View Details" button. For round-trip searches, outbound and return flights are displayed in separate sections or tabs. The left sidebar contains filter sections: Airlines (checkboxes with airline names and logos), Stops (checkboxes for Non-stop, 1 Stop, 2+ Stops), Departure Time (time range slider for morning, afternoon, evening, night), Arrival Time (time range slider), and Price Range (dual-handle slider). Sorting options at the top include "Cheapest", "Fastest", "Best" (balanced), "Departure Time", and "Arrival Time". Filters update results dynamically. Clicking "View Details" on a flight expands the card to show additional information including flight number, aircraft type, baggage allowance (cabin and checked), fare class rules (refundable/non-refundable, change fees), seat availability indicator, and layover details for connecting flights showing intermediate airports and wait times.
Functional Overview.8. Flight Booking: The flight booking page is accessed after selecting a flight from the listing page. The page displays a flight summary section at the top showing the complete itinerary: departure and arrival cities, airports, dates, times, airline, flight number, duration, and class for each leg of the journey. For round-trip flights, both outbound and return flights are displayed. Below the summary, a fare breakdown section shows the base fare per passenger, taxes and surcharges itemized, and the total fare. The passenger information form collects details for each traveler with fields for Title (dropdown: Mr, Mrs, Ms, Miss, Dr), First Name (text input, required), Last Name (text input, required), Date of Birth (date picker with day/month/year dropdowns, required), Nationality (country dropdown), Passport Number (text input, required for international flights), Passport Expiry Date (date picker, must be at least 6 months from travel date), and Contact Information for the lead passenger including Email (required) and Phone Number with country code selector (required). Additional options include Special Requests (textarea for dietary, wheelchair, or other needs), Meal Preferences (dropdown: Standard, Vegetarian, Vegan, Halal, Kosher), and Seat Selection (if available, displaying a seat map with available seats in green and occupied seats in gray). All required fields show red validation messages if left empty or formatted incorrectly (e.g., "First name is required", "Invalid passport number format", "Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from travel date"). A Terms and Conditions checkbox must be accepted. A "Continue" or "Proceed to Payment" button at the bottom proceeds to the payment page.
Functional Overview.9. Tours Search & Listing: The tours listing page is accessed via the "Tours" link in the navigation or after submitting a tour search. The page displays available tour packages in a card layout. Each tour card shows an attractive banner image, the tour name as a bold clickable heading, the destination location with a map pin icon, the duration displayed as "X Days / Y Nights" (e.g., "5 Days / 4 Nights"), a brief description or highlights summary (first 2-3 lines), the starting price per person displayed prominently (e.g., "From $599"), an availability indicator ("Available" in green or "Limited Spots" in orange), an aggregate guest rating (star icons with score and review count), and a "View Details" button. The left sidebar contains filter sections: Destination (checkboxes for available destinations with result counts), Tour Type (checkboxes for Adventure, Cultural, Cruise, Wildlife, Beach, City Tour, etc.), Price Range (dual-handle slider with minimum and maximum amounts), Duration (checkboxes for ranges like "1-3 Days", "4-7 Days", "8-14 Days", "15+ Days"), and Departure Dates (calendar-based date range selector). Sorting options at the top include "Popularity", "Price: Low to High", "Price: High to Low", "Duration", and "Rating". Filters update results dynamically without page reload, and the total result count updates (e.g., "12 tours found"). Pagination appears at the bottom for results spanning multiple pages.
Functional Overview.10. Tour Details & Booking: The tour details page is accessed by clicking a tour name or "View Details" from the listing. Breadcrumbs show the navigation path (Home > Tours > [Destination] > [Tour Name]). At the top, a slideshow or photo gallery displays multiple tour images with navigation arrows and thumbnail indicators. The tour name appears as a large heading with the destination, duration, and aggregate rating. A detailed Itinerary section provides a day-by-day breakdown with expandable/collapsible day panels (Day 1, Day 2, etc.), each describing activities, meals, transportation, and accommodation for that day. An Inclusions section lists what is covered (accommodation, selected meals, guided tours, entrance fees, transportation between destinations) with green checkmark icons. An Exclusions section lists what is not covered (international flights, travel insurance, personal expenses, tips) with red X icons. A Departure Dates section displays a table or calendar showing available departure dates, remaining spots, and prices, with "Select" buttons for each date. A Pricing section shows the per-person cost with breakdowns for Adults and Children (with age-based pricing), group discounts if applicable, and any seasonal surcharges. A Guest Reviews section displays individual reviews with ratings, reviewer information, and comments. A Terms & Conditions section covers cancellation policy, travel insurance requirements, and booking conditions. The booking form collects traveler information: number of Adults and Children (with age specification), selected departure date, lead traveler details (First Name, Last Name, Email, Phone), special requirements (textarea), and displays the total cost breakdown. A "Book Now" button proceeds to the payment page after validation.
Functional Overview.11. Cars Search & Listing: The car rental listing page is displayed after submitting a car search. The top section shows the search summary (pick-up location, drop-off location, dates and times) with an "Edit Search" option. The main content area lists available vehicles, each displayed as a card showing the vehicle image on the left, the make and model name as a bold heading (e.g., "Toyota Corolla" or "BMW 3 Series"), vehicle category label (Economy, Compact, Midsize, SUV, Luxury, Van), icons with labels for key features: transmission type (Automatic/Manual with gear icon), fuel policy (Full-to-Full, Same-to-Same), seating capacity (number of passengers with person icon), luggage capacity (number of bags with suitcase icon), air conditioning (AC icon), and optional features (GPS, Bluetooth). The rental company name and logo are displayed. Pricing shows the daily rate and total rental cost for the selected period prominently on the right side. A "Book Now" or "View Details" button appears for each vehicle. The left sidebar contains filter sections: Car Type (checkboxes for Economy, Compact, Midsize, Full-size, SUV, Luxury, Van, Convertible with result counts), Transmission (checkboxes for Automatic, Manual), Fuel Policy (checkboxes for Full-to-Full, Same-to-Same), Rental Company (checkboxes with company names and logos), and Price Range (dual-handle slider). Sorting options include "Price: Low to High", "Price: High to Low", and "Recommended". Users can compare multiple vehicles by selecting a "Compare" checkbox on up to 3-4 vehicles, which opens a comparison table showing specifications side by side.
Functional Overview.12. Car Booking: The car booking page is accessed after selecting a vehicle from the listing. The page displays a vehicle summary section showing the selected car image, make and model, category, key features icons, and the rental company name. A rental period summary shows pick-up and drop-off locations (with addresses), dates and times. The pricing breakdown section shows the daily rate, number of rental days, subtotal, selected add-ons costs, insurance costs, taxes, and total amount. The driver information form collects First Name (required), Last Name (required), Age (required, minimum age restrictions apply), Driver's License Number (required), License Issue Country (dropdown, required), Email (required), and Phone Number with country code (required). An Insurance & Add-ons section presents optional selections with prices: Collision Damage Waiver (CDW), Theft Protection, Full Insurance Coverage, GPS Navigation Device, Child Seat (with age specification), Additional Driver (with name fields), and Roadside Assistance. Each option has a checkbox with its daily or total price displayed. Terms and Conditions sections cover fuel policy details (e.g., "Return the car with a full tank"), mileage limits (unlimited or daily cap with excess charges), damage liability and deposit information, late return charges, cancellation policy with refund percentages based on timing (e.g., "Free cancellation up to 48 hours before pick-up"), and age-related surcharges. A Terms and Conditions checkbox must be accepted. A "Confirm Booking" button proceeds to the payment page after validating all required fields.
Functional Overview.13. Visa Services: The Visa section is accessed via the "Visa" link in the top navigation bar. The page displays a form at the top with two dropdown selectors: Nationality (list of countries the traveler is from) and Destination (list of countries the traveler wants to visit). A "Check Requirements" or "Search" button submits the selection. The results section displays visa requirement details including visa type required (Tourist Visa, Business Visa, Transit Visa, E-Visa, Visa on Arrival, or Visa Free), processing time (e.g., "5-7 business days"), validity period (e.g., "30 days", "90 days"), required documents list (valid passport with minimum 6 months validity, completed application form, passport-sized photographs with specifications, proof of accommodation, return flight tickets, invitation letter if applicable, bank statements for financial proof, travel insurance certificate), and visa fees displayed in the selected currency. The visa application form, if the service is fully functional, collects personal information: Full Name, Date of Birth (date picker), Nationality, Passport Number, Passport Issue Date, Passport Expiry Date, Place of Birth, and Contact Information (email, phone, address). Travel details fields include Purpose of Visit (dropdown: Tourism, Business, Education, Medical, Transit), Intended Travel Dates (date range picker), Duration of Stay, Accommodation Details (hotel name and address), and Point of Entry. A document upload section allows uploading required paperwork with drag-and-drop areas or file browse buttons for each document type (passport copy, photos, invitation letter, etc.), showing accepted file formats (PDF, JPG, PNG) and maximum file sizes. A submission button sends the application. Authenticated users can track their visa application status through their account dashboard showing stages like Submitted, Under Review, Additional Documents Required, Approved, and Rejected, with estimated completion dates.
Functional Overview.14. User Dashboard: After login, the user dashboard is accessed by clicking the user name dropdown in the navigation and selecting "Dashboard", or by navigating directly to the dashboard URL. The dashboard layout features a left sidebar navigation menu with links for My Bookings, My Profile, Wallet, Wishlist, Reviews, Settings, and Logout. The main content area changes based on the selected sidebar item. The My Bookings section (default view) displays all past and upcoming bookings organized by tabs or filters for All, Hotels, Flights, Tours, and Cars. Each booking card shows the booking reference number, the service type icon, the property/airline/tour/car name and image, booking dates, traveler count, booking status badge (Confirmed in green, Pending in yellow, Cancelled in red, Completed in gray), the total amount paid, and action buttons including "View Details" (opens full booking details), "Cancel" (initiates cancellation flow), "Modify" (allows date or detail changes), and "Download" options for invoice, voucher, or confirmation PDF. The My Profile section displays personal information (name, email, phone, address, nationality), passport details (number, issue date, expiry date, issuing country), and an "Edit" button that opens editable fields. The Wallet section shows the current balance, transaction history table (date, type, amount, description), and options to add credits. The Wishlist section displays saved hotels, tours, or flights as cards with quick "Remove" and "Book Now" buttons. The Reviews section lists completed bookings eligible for review with "Write Review" buttons, and shows previously submitted reviews with ratings and text. The Settings section contains Password Change (current password, new password, confirm new password fields with an "Update" button), Notification Preferences (toggles for email notifications for booking confirmations, reminders, promotions), and Currency/Language default preferences.
Functional Overview.15. Booking Management: Booking management functionality is available within the dashboard's My Bookings section or via email confirmation links that include a direct booking reference URL. For each booking, the detailed view page displays the complete booking information: booking reference number prominently at the top, booking status with a colored badge, service details (hotel name/room type, or flight itinerary, or tour package, or car rental details), traveler information (names, contact details, passport info for flights), check-in/check-out or travel dates, a price breakdown table (base price, taxes, fees, discounts applied, total paid), payment method used (last 4 digits of card or payment method name), and booking timestamps (booked on, last modified). The Modify option (available for eligible bookings based on the cancellation/modification policy) allows changing dates using date pickers that show updated availability and price differences, updating traveler information fields, adding special requests, and shows any modification fees. A "Confirm Changes" button applies modifications and sends a confirmation email. The Cancel option opens a cancellation flow that first displays the cancellation policy applicable to the booking (e.g., "Free cancellation until [date]", "50% refund if cancelled between [date range]", "Non-refundable after [date]"), shows the calculated refund amount based on the policy, requires the user to confirm cancellation via a "Confirm Cancellation" button with an optional reason dropdown and comments textarea. Upon confirmed cancellation, the system processes the refund to the original payment method, updates the booking status to "Cancelled", and sends a cancellation confirmation email with refund details and expected processing time (e.g., "5-10 business days").
Functional Overview.16. Payment Processing: The payment page is the final step before completing any booking (hotel, flight, tour, or car). The page displays a comprehensive booking summary section at the top showing all selected services, dates, traveler count, and a detailed price breakdown table: base price, taxes (itemized by type), service fees, any applied discount codes or promotional offers, and the total amount due in the selected currency with a conversion note if applicable. A Promo Code input field with an "Apply" button allows entering discount codes, which updates the total when valid or shows an error for invalid/expired codes. The payment methods section displays available options as selectable cards or radio buttons: Credit/Debit Card (with logos for Visa, MasterCard, American Express, and Discover), PayPal (with PayPal logo and redirect notice), Bank Transfer (with bank details and reference instructions), and Wallet/Credits (showing available balance with "Use Credits" option). When Credit/Debit Card is selected, the form displays fields for Cardholder Name (text input, must match card), Card Number (text input with auto-formatting showing spaces every 4 digits, card type logo auto-detected from the number prefix — Visa starts with 4, MasterCard with 5, AmEx with 3), Expiration Date (MM/YY format with dropdowns or formatted input), and CVV (3-digit input, 4 digits for AmEx, masked). Real-time validation provides instant feedback: green checkmark for valid fields, red error messages for invalid inputs (e.g., "Invalid card number", "Card has expired"). A "Save this card for future use" checkbox is optionally available. Security indicators include SSL encryption badge, PCI compliance logo, and secure connection indicator. A Terms and Conditions checkbox must be accepted before payment. A prominent "Pay Now" or "Complete Booking" button submits the payment. During processing, a loading spinner or progress indicator is displayed. Upon successful payment, a booking confirmation page appears showing a green checkmark icon, "Booking Confirmed!" heading, the booking reference number in large text, booking summary details, and buttons for "Download Invoice", "Download Voucher", "View Booking", and "Return to Home". A confirmation email is automatically sent. If payment fails, an error message appears (e.g., "Payment declined. Please check your card details and try again." or "Insufficient funds.") with options to retry with the same method or select a different payment method.
Functional Overview.17. Currency & Language Selection: The currency and language selectors are located in the top navigation bar, visible on every page. The currency selector is a dropdown button displaying the currently selected currency code and flag (e.g., "USD" with a US flag icon). Clicking it reveals a dropdown list of available currencies, each showing the country flag, currency code, and currency name (e.g., "USD - US Dollar", "EUR - Euro", "GBP - British Pound", "AED - UAE Dirham", "SAR - Saudi Riyal", "INR - Indian Rupee"). Selecting a different currency immediately updates all prices displayed throughout the site — on listing pages, detail pages, booking pages, and the dashboard — in real-time without losing the current page context, search results, or cart items. The conversion uses the system's configured exchange rates. The language selector is a similar dropdown displaying the current language name or flag. Clicking it reveals available languages (English, Arabic, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Turkish, etc.) with their respective flag icons. Selecting a different language switches the entire interface text — navigation labels, button text, form labels, descriptions, error messages — to the selected language, with right-to-left (RTL) layout automatically applied for Arabic and similar languages. These preferences are stored in browser cookies/session for unauthenticated users and persist across pages during the session. For authenticated users, currency and language preferences are saved to their user profile and applied automatically on future logins.
Functional Overview.18. Search & Filters: All listing pages (hotels, flights, tours, cars) share a consistent filtering and sorting architecture. The left sidebar contains collapsible filter sections, each with a section heading and a toggle arrow to expand or collapse the filter group. Common filter types include: Price Range displayed as a dual-handle slider with minimum and maximum values, allowing users to drag handles to set a custom price range with the filtered count updating dynamically; Star Rating or Quality Rating displayed as checkboxes with star icons and the count of matching results in parentheses; Amenities/Features displayed as a scrollable checkbox list with facility names, icons, and result counts, with a "Show More" link if the list exceeds a certain number; Location/Area (for hotels) displayed as checkboxes with neighborhood or district names; Departure/Arrival Times (for flights) displayed as time range sliders divided into Morning, Afternoon, Evening, and Night segments; Duration (for tours) displayed as checkbox ranges; Vehicle Type (for cars) displayed as checkboxes with vehicle category icons. As users select or deselect any filter, the results area updates dynamically without a full page reload, using AJAX requests to fetch filtered data. The total result count at the top of the listing updates to reflect filtered results (e.g., "Showing 8 of 25 hotels"). At the top of the results area, active filter tags appear as removable badges/chips — clicking the X on any tag removes that filter and updates results. A "Clear All Filters" button resets all filters to their default state. Sorting controls appear as a dropdown at the top of the results with options specific to each listing type. The listing dynamically reloads results based on the selected sort order.
Functional Overview.19. Reviews & Ratings: Reviews and ratings are displayed throughout the site on listing pages and detail pages for hotels, tours, and cars. On listing pages, each item card shows an aggregate rating displayed as a numerical score (e.g., "8.5" or "4.5/5") with a colored badge (dark green for excellent, green for very good, yellow for good, orange for fair), the descriptive rating label ("Excellent", "Very Good", "Good", "Fair"), and the total number of reviews in parentheses (e.g., "(127 reviews)"). On detail pages, a dedicated Reviews section displays: an aggregate overview panel showing the overall score prominently, category-specific breakdown scores with horizontal progress bars (Cleanliness, Service, Location, Comfort, Facilities, Value for Money), and the total review count. Below the overview, individual review cards are displayed, each containing the reviewer's first name and country flag, the review date and stay/travel date, a personal rating score, a detailed comment text area, and optionally uploaded photos displayed as clickable thumbnails that open in a lightbox. Review filtering options allow sorting by Most Recent, Highest Rating, Lowest Rating, and filtering by Traveler Type (Families, Couples, Solo, Business). A "Load More Reviews" button or pagination loads additional reviews. Authenticated users who have completed bookings can submit reviews through the dashboard or via post-stay/trip email prompts. The review submission form displays the booking reference and destination, star rating selectors or sliders for each category (all required), a text area for detailed feedback comments (minimum character count enforced, e.g., "Minimum 50 characters"), an optional photo upload area (drag-and-drop or browse, accepting JPG/PNG, maximum 5 photos), and a "Submit Review" button. Submitted reviews may undergo moderation before appearing publicly.
Functional Overview.20. Offers & Deals: The Offers page is accessed via the "Offers" link in the top navigation bar. The page displays a hero banner at the top showcasing the current top promotion with a large background image, promotional text (e.g., "Summer Sale: Up to 40% Off Hotels"), validity dates, and a "Book Now" or "View Deals" call-to-action button. Below the hero banner, a filter bar allows filtering offers by category tabs (All, Hotels, Flights, Packages, Last Minute), destination dropdown, and travel date range picker. The main content area displays offer cards in a grid layout, each showing an attractive destination or property image, the deal title in bold (e.g., "Early Bird Discount", "Flash Sale", "Weekend Getaway Package"), the discount badge displayed prominently (e.g., "30% OFF" in a red or orange badge overlay on the image), the original price crossed out with the discounted price displayed next to it, a brief description of the offer terms, the validity period ("Valid until [date]"), a "Terms & Conditions" expandable link that reveals the full offer details (minimum stay requirements, blackout dates, applicable room types or routes, non-combinable clauses), and a prominent "Book Now" button. Clicking "Book Now" either applies the promotional discount code automatically and redirects to the relevant search results with discounted rates pre-applied, or takes the user to a pre-filled booking page with the offer already selected. Featured or time-sensitive deals may display a countdown timer showing days, hours, minutes, and seconds remaining. At the bottom of the page, a newsletter subscription section contains a heading like "Don't miss out on exclusive deals!", an email input field with placeholder text, and a "Subscribe" button that registers the email for promotional emails and displays a confirmation message "Thank you for subscribing!"
Functional Overview.Logout: The logout function is accessible from two locations: the user name dropdown in the top navigation bar which displays a "Logout" link at the bottom of the dropdown menu, and from the dashboard sidebar menu which lists "Logout" as the last item. When the user clicks Logout from either location, the system terminates the authenticated user session on the server, clears all session cookies and locally stored authentication tokens from the browser, and redirects the user to the home page with the navigation bar reverting to show "Login" and "Signup" links instead of the user dropdown. A brief confirmation message may appear as a toast notification: "You have been successfully logged out." After logout, any attempt to access protected pages (such as the dashboard, bookings, profile, or active booking flows) redirects the user to the login page with a message: "Please log in to access this page." The shopping cart or search criteria from the previous session may be cleared depending on server-side session configuration, though unauthenticated search functionality remains accessible.
